title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Importance Of Medical Test In Term Insurance
Why You Should Undergo Medical Tests Before Buying Term Insurance
Undergoing a medical test before purchasing a term policy plays a significant role. Ideally, these medical tests are essential for the determination of your health quotient. When a policyholder gets a medical examination done beforehand, the insurance companies provide them with customized plans based on such reports. Failure to do so could lead to undesirable repercussions in the long run.
Before you buy a term insurance, go through the importance of undergoing a medical test mentioned below. Take a look:
Importance of medical criteria
Insurance companies decide how much you pay based on the risks they see. A medical test helps find out about your health before you buy a policy. This lets the company understand your condition better and set the right price for your insurance. Medical tests can be different for each person, but usually include things like blood tests, urine tests, and weight checks. These tests depend on the information you give in your application form. Doing a medical test has many benefits. It helps the insurance company know your health clearly. It also helps you get the right coverage at a fair price. By checking your health first, both you and the insurance company can be sure about the plan. Medical tests make the process clearer and safer for everyone involved.
When is it required to have a medical test to buy a term insurance plan?
A medical test is usually needed when buying a term insurance plan if you are 40 years old or older. These tests help the insurance company understand your health better. The standard tests include blood tests, urine tests, and weight and height measurements. The results help the insurance company decide how much you will have to pay and how long the coverage will last.
For younger people, medical tests may not usually be necessary. However, coverage from plans without medical tests may be less or cost more. The medical tests are important for insurance companies because they provide them with a better understanding of your health, which helps them provide you with a fair price and coverage that matches your health.
Generally, medical tests are beneficial for you as well as the insurer because they ensure that you have a policy that matches your health and finances, and helps to avoid unpleasant surprises in the future. If you're 40 or older, have a medical test as part of the process of getting term insurance.
Benefits of undergoing a medical test before purchasing term insurance:
1. Your claims are approved
The health condition of a policyholder significantly impacts the claims. Hence, take a medical test since you’ll have to make a statement about the same in the application form. In case you keep your existing health condition under wraps, it will lead to future consequences. Failure to disclose your medical history could result in rejection of the claim.
2. You pay a lower premium
Credible insurance companies make these medical tests a mandate. A policy that requires medical tests bases their premium rate on the results of the medical reports. For instance, an excellent medical health report means lesser premiums and vice versa.
3. Insurance companies provide extensive coverage
Typically, the companies that do not require medical reports for low-value plans. Usually insurance companies do not provide its policyholders with a coverage of more than five lakhs without medical clearance. To obtain a sustainable amount of coverage, get your medical tests done, and let the insurance company take the decision further. Opting for a broader coverage paves the way to achieving your life goals with ease.
To sum up, a medical test can impact the premium amounts under term policies. It not only gives a clear picture of your health but also helps approve any future claims. Hence, go ahead with the medical tests first as a backup for the heath declaration to be made in the proposal form, and then make a purchase, accordingly.
Which medical tests are required for term insurance?
When buying a term insurance plan, many companies ask for medical tests. These tests help the insurance company understand your health. This is important because your health can affect how much you pay and how much coverage you get.
Usually, people who are older or those asking for high coverage are required to take medical tests. These tests are done to check if there are any health problems. The test results help the company know more about your current condition. This makes it easier for them to decide on the policy terms.
The tests are simple and safe. They may take only a few minutes. Most of them are like regular check-ups you may have done before. These tests also help you know more about your health.
The tests may be different depending on your age, lifestyle, or the company’s rules. But most people taking term insurance will be asked to do a few basic tests.
Common Medical Tests for Term Insurance
Blood Test – To check sugar levels, cholesterol, and other health markers.
Urine Test – To look for signs of kidney issues or infections.
BMI Check – To measure your body weight against your height.
Blood Pressure Check – To see if your blood pressure is too high or too low.
ECG (Electrocardiogram) – To check your heart’s rhythm.
Chest X-Ray – To see your heart and lungs.
HIV Test – To check for HIV infection.
Treadmill Test (TMT) – To see how your heart works when walking or running.
Liver Function Test – To check if your liver is healthy.
Kidney Function Test – To see how well your kidneys work.
Ultrasound (USG) – To take images of the inside organs.
Medical Tests and Their Purpose
Why is it beneficial to have a medical test for your term plan?
A medical examination assists the insurer in gathering a better understanding of your health. This is to ensure the coverage in your plan is appropriate for your situation, which also helps prevent future problems and may even help you get a lower premium. If you are required to make a claim, they will also help clarify your claims process. Medical examinations provide clarity and equity for both you and the insurer
Prevent Claim Rejection
Medical tests help show your true health before buying insurance. This prevents claim problems later. If you hide health issues, claims might be denied. Tests help insurance companies trust your health information. This makes it easier to get the money your family needs if something happens.
Evaluate the Client's Risk of Death
Medical tests let the insurer check your health risks. They look at your age, habits, and medical history. This helps them decide how risky it is to insure you. The better they understand your health, the fairer your premium and coverage will be.
Low-Cost Premiums
Good health shown by medical tests, can lower your premium. Healthier people usually pay less. Tests help prove your health to the insurer. This way, you can get protection at a price that fits your budget.
Changes in Coverage
Medical test results can change your policy terms. If health problems are found, coverage might be limited or premiums might rise. These changes make sure your policy matches your health situation and risks.
Better Policy Customisation
Knowing your health through tests helps insurers create policies just for you. They can adjust or add coverage to meet your needs. This makes your insurance more useful and gives you better protection.
Quicker Claim Process
Conducting the medical tests early allows claims to be done much quicker. The insurers already have your health information. This allows them to verify a claim and pay your family a lot quicker when they are needed.
Procedure for medical test for term insurance cover
When buying a term insurance plan, a medical test may be needed. This helps the insurance company understand your health before giving you coverage. Usually, if you are 40 or older or want a higher amount of insurance, you will need to take these tests. The process begins with an application as well as a health assessment questionnaire; once that is complete, the insurer will schedule a medical examination, which can be done either at a clinic or in your own home. These tests are as simple as a blood test, a urine sample, taking your blood pressure, measuring your height and weight, and assessing your heart. Sometimes, the company will also have a chest X-ray ordered and sometimes tests for infectious disease. Once the company reviews the results of the tests and your health history, they decide how much you should pay for your premiums as well as what you are covered for. The underwriting processes not only helps the company to determine a fair price for your premiums, but it also provides the insurer to assess your coverage needs.
FAQs
Who will pay for my medical tests?
The cost of medical tests for term insurance is usually covered by the insurance company. These tests are part of the application process and are arranged by the insurer at approved labs or, in some cases, through home visits. This means applicants generally do not have to pay for the tests themselves, making it easier to complete the health assessment needed for the policy.
Is the medical test mandatory?
Medical tests are required for many term insurance applicants, especially if the person is older or wants a larger coverage amount. For younger people or those choosing lower coverage, tests may not be needed. The insurance provider decides whether a medical examination is necessary based on age, health information, and the amount of insurance requested.
Do medical history or records have to be submitted to the insurance company?
The applicant applies for the term insurance policy by providing any relevant medical histories or health records. This information will allow the insurance company to assess their health risks correctly and accurately. Accurate disclosure helps bail the company to offer a policy with accurate coverage and pricing for the applicant based on their health.
Are there any hidden terms or conditions that I should know about?
Insurance policies will contain important terms regarding coverage, disclosures of health, and the claims process. There are normally no hidden costs, but it is important to read the entire policy wording in its entirety. The terms and conditions help to clarify expectations by all parties, the insured and the insurer, to manage any unwanted surprises later!
Do I have to visit the diagnostic centre to undergo a medical test?
You don't always have to go to a diagnostic centre to do your medical test. Many insurers provide a home medical exam, where the healthcare professional comes to you to conduct the tests that you are required to undergo. However, in some instances, you may have to go to a diagnostic centre, especially if a home-based medical test is not available or if there are additional tests that are required.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Why You Should Undergo Medical Tests Before Buying Term Insurance
Undergoing a medical test before purchasing a term policy plays a significant role. Ideally, these medical tests are essential for the determination of your health quotient. When a policyholder gets a medical examination done beforehand, the insurance companies provide them with customized plans based on such reports. Failure to do so could lead to undesirable repercussions in the long run.
Before you buy a term insurance, go through the importance of undergoing a medical test mentioned
below. Take a look:
Importance of medical tests

Medical tests can be different for each person, but usually include tests like blood tests, urine tests, BMI test etc. These tests depend on the information you give in your application form. Doing a medical test has many benefits. It informs the insurance company of any underlying medical condition. It also helps you get the right coverage at a fair price.

Generally, medical tests are beneficial for you as well as the insurer because they ensure that you have a policy that matches your health and finances, and helps to avoid unpleasant surprises in the future. If you're 40 or older, having a medical test is a part of the process of getting term insurance.
Benefits of undergoing a medical test before purchasing term insurance:
1. Your claims are approved
The health condition of a policyholder significantly impacts the claims. Hence, take a medical test since
you’ll have to make a statement about the same in the application form. In case you keep your existing
health condition under wraps, it will lead to future consequences. Failure to disclose your medical history

report means lesser premiums and vice versa.

Typically, companiesdo not require medical reports for low-value plans. Usually insurance companies do

a sustainable amount of coverage, get your medical tests done, and let the insurance company take the
decision further. Opting for a broader coverage paves the way to achieving your life goals with ease.

first as a backup for the heath declaration to be made in the proposal form, and then make a purchase,
accordingly.

When buying a term insurance plan, many companies ask for medical tests. These tests help the insurance company understand your health. This is important because your health can affect your premium and how much coverage you get.
Usually, people who are older or those asking for high coverage are required to take medical tests. These tests are done to assess your health. The test results help the company know more about your current health condition. This makes it easier for them to decide on the policy terms.
The tests may be different depending on your age, lifestyle, insurer etc . But most people taking term insurance will be asked to do a few basic tests.
Common Medical Tests for Term Insurance
● Blood Test – To check blood sugar levels, cholesterol, and other health markers. ● Urine Test – To diagnose any kidney related condition or other health markers.. ● BMI Check – To measure your body weight against your height. ● Blood Pressure Test – To check blood pressure and diagnose any related conditions. ● ECG (Electrocardiogram) – To assess your heart’s activity and diagnose any related condition. ● Chest X-Ray – To assess the condition of your heart and lungs. ● HIV Test – To check for HIV infection. ● Treadmill Test (TMT) – To evaluate your heart’s response to different stimuli’s. ● Liver Function Test – To check the functioning of your liver . ● Kidney Function Test – To check the functioning of your kidney. ● Ultrasound (USG) – To produce real time images of internal organs for further diagnosis , if any..
